steps.title: Determine the frequencies from both histograms
steps.content:
Reading the bar heights in each histogram:

Data Set A:
[10, 20): frequency = 3
[20, 30): frequency = 4
[30, 40): frequency = 7
[40, 50): frequency = 9
Total = 23 ✓

Data Set B:
[10, 20): frequency = 3
[20, 30): frequency = 4
[30, 40): frequency = 7
[40, 50): frequency = 9
Total = 23 ✓

Since the mean = (sum of all values) / 23, we need to find which integer assignments within each interval minimize the difference between the two means.

steps.title: Find the minimum sum for each data set
steps.content:
To minimize each sum, assign the lowest value in each interval:

$$\text{Min sum (both sets)} = 3(10) + 4(20) + 7(30) + 9(40)$$
$$= 30 + 80 + 210 + 360 = 680$$

steps.title: Find the maximum sum for each data set
steps.content:
To maximize each sum, assign the highest value in each interval (respecting the upper bound is exclusive):

$$\text{Max sum (both sets)} = 3(19) + 4(29) + 7(39) + 9(49)$$
$$= 57 + 116 + 273 + 441 = 887$$

steps.title: Minimize the difference in means
steps.content:
Since both sets have identical frequency distributions, the possible sums for both range from 680 to 887. To minimize the difference in means, assign the same sum to both sets.

When both sets have sum = 680 (or any other equal value):
$$\text{Mean}_A = \frac{680}{23}, \quad \text{Mean}_B = \frac{680}{23}$$
$$\text{Difference} = \frac{680}{23} - \frac{680}{23} = 0$$

This smallest possible difference matches choice A — A is correct.
